headline.recommendation: Do not wire Numbas records into app code directly. Build an offline Numbas-to-static-variant conversion pipeline, then import only reviewed generated fixed-choice cards.
headline.directImportReady: Effectively no assessed direct-import subset in the current extraction because answer/correctness configuration is omitted.

fixedChoiceCandidateSubset.definition: Permissive CC BY / CC BY-SA records whose part types are only Numbas choice-like parts: 1_n_2, m_n_2, m_n_x, yes-no, true-false.
fixedChoiceCandidateSubset.note: These still need full .exam refetch plus Numbas parameter rendering to recover option text, correct choices, feedback, and deterministic generated variants.

conversionStages: Refetch full .exam payload for selected records.
Apply licence and route gates before rendering.
Run Numbas variable generation with deterministic seeds.
Render statement, advice, LaTeX, variables, choice text, correct answers, and simple generated assets.
Convert one rendered variant into a Floe fixed-choice candidate with provenance and review status.
Review for answer leakage, diagram dependency, distractor quality, and target course fit before app import.

candidateFamilies.family: BODMAS / order of operations multiple choice
candidateFamilies.reason: Choice-like arithmetic rules can become fixed-choice cards after option/correctness refetch and variable rendering.
Fixed-choice if rendered graphs are static assets; hold GeoGebra-heavy variants.
Multiple-select factor identification maps naturally to fixed-choice after option extraction.
Single-choice procedural algebra with small variable set.
Matching/classification choices can become fixed-choice or multi-select cards with rendered matrices.
True/false statements are the closest shape to current fixed-choice cards.
Choice conversion is straightforward, but graph diagrams must be rendered as static assets.
Matching logical statements to symbolic forms is fixed-choice friendly.
Classification choices produce useful statistics concept checks.
Classification of event pairs can be flattened into multiple fixed-choice cards.
Good conceptual fit, but some records have missing current route metadata and need source answer fields.
Rendered symbolic options can become a single fixed-choice calculus card.
Works if plots are rendered to static images and option text is recovered.
Currently gapfill numeric, but can be transformed into generated fixed-choice numeric cards using plausible distractors.
Number-entry probabilities need computed distractors; not a direct fixed-choice conversion.
High-value target family, but frequently uses applets/GeoGebra and gapfill numeric answers.
